summary: Ran the 5-minute AgentOps scheduler cycle and added a Run Manager persistence semantics policy for gate provenance, Big @ reference audit candidates, typed handoff provenance, eval snapshots, and audit export packages. These helper outputs are now contractually read-only until mapped back to established Aletheia source records.
testsRun: node .agentops/scripts/check-agentops.mjs (failed before normalization: .agentops/status/gate-engine.json used noncanonical status "working")
node .agentops/scripts/check-agentops.mjs (pre-change: passed with architecture-contracts legacy warning)
node .agentops/scripts/check-agentops.mjs (passed with zero warnings after architecture-contracts canonical normalization)
node .agentops/scripts/check-agentops.mjs (passed after adding Run Manager persistenceSemanticsPolicy validation)
risks: Workflow Scheduler normalized the last observed legacy status shape; future feature-agent status files may still reintroduce legacy fields if owners do not follow AGENT_STATUS_SCHEMA.md.
The run trace is a deterministic local simulation and is not yet wired to persisted Aletheia matter records through the adapter.
The new persistence semantics policy is an orchestration contract only; it does not add backend persistence, migrations, or API behavior.
needs: Adapter agent should map persisted Aletheia matter/run records into this workflow trace shape before UI treats it as product truth.
